Ted Carroll, Roger Armstrong, Trevor Churchill

[newspapers](https://www.google.com/search?&q=%22Ace+Records%22+United+Kingdom&tbs=bkt:s&tbm=bks) **·** [books](https://www.google.com/search?tbs=bks:1&q=%22Ace+Records%22+United+Kingdom+-wikipedia) **·** [scholar](https://scholar.google.com/scholar?q=%22Ace+Records%22+United+Kingdom) **·** [JSTOR](https://www.jstor.org/action/doBasicSearch?Query=%22Ace+Records%22+United+Kingdom&acc=on&wc=on) *(July 2016)* *([Learn how and when to remove this message](/wiki/Help:Maintenance_template_removal "Help:Maintenance template removal"))* |

is a British record label founded in 1978.[[1]](#cite_note-1) Initially the company only gained permission from the [similarly named label](/wiki/Ace_Records_(United_States) "Ace Records (United States)") based in Mississippi to use the name in the UK, but eventually also acquired the rights to publish their recordings.[[2]](#cite_note-acerecords1-2) When [Chiswick Records](/wiki/Chiswick_Records "Chiswick Records")' pop side was licensed to EMI in [1984](/wiki/1984_in_music "1984 in music"), Ace switched to more licensing and reissuing work. In the 1980s it also gained the licensing for [Modern Records](/wiki/Modern_Records "Modern Records"),[[2]](#cite_note-acerecords1-2) and its follow-up company [Kent Records](/wiki/Kent_Records "Kent Records"), whilst in the 1990s, the company bought the labels including all original master tapes.

initially the only label[[4]](#cite_note-4) (reissues, also including [Ace Records (US)](/wiki/Ace_Records_(US) "Ace Records (US)")): Rock 'n' Roll, Rockabilly, Rhythm & Blues, Cajun, 1950s and 1960s Pop, etc. It is well known for several series of releases: "The Golden Age of American Rock 'N' Roll"; "Early Girls"; "Teen Beat" (showcasing instrumentals); and "Radio Gold".

From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

British record label

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| [icon](/wiki/File:Question_book-new.svg) | This article **needs additional citations for [verification](/wiki/Wikipedia:Verifiability "Wikipedia:Verifiability")**. Unsourced material may be challenged and removed. *Find sources:* ["Ace Records" United Kingdom](https://www.google.com/search?as_eq=wikipedia&q=%22Ace+Records%22+United+Kingdom) – [news](https://www.google.com/search?tbm=nws&q=%22Ace+Records%22+United+Kingdom+-wikipedia&tbs=ar:1) **·** [newspapers](https://www.google.com/search?&q=%22Ace+Records%22+United+Kingdom&tbs=bkt:s&tbm=bks) **·** [books](https://www.google.com/search?tbs=bks:1&q=%22Ace+Records%22+United+Kingdom+-wikipedia) **·** [scholar](https://scholar.google.com/scholar?q=%22Ace+Records%22+United+Kingdom) **·** [JSTOR](https://www.jstor.org/action/doBasicSearch?Query=%22Ace+Records%22+United+Kingdom&acc=on&wc=on) *(July 2016)* *([Learn how and when to remove this message](/wiki/Help:Maintenance_template_removal "Help:Maintenance template removal"))* |

Record label

| Ace Records | |
| --- | --- |
|  | |
| Parent company | Ace Records Ltd. |
| Founded | 1978 (1978) |
| Founder | Ted Carroll, Roger Armstrong, Trevor Churchill |
| Genre | Various, reissues |
| Country of origin | England |
| Location | London |
| Official website | [www.acerecords.co.uk](http://www.acerecords.co.uk) |

**Ace Records Ltd.** is a British record label founded in 1978.[[1]](#cite_note-1) Initially the company only gained permission from the [similarly named label](/wiki/Ace_Records_(United_States) "Ace Records (United States)") based in Mississippi to use the name in the UK, but eventually also acquired the rights to publish their recordings.[[2]](#cite_note-acerecords1-2) When [Chiswick Records](/wiki/Chiswick_Records "Chiswick Records")' pop side was licensed to EMI in [1984](/wiki/1984_in_music "1984 in music"), Ace switched to more licensing and reissuing work. In the 1980s it also gained the licensing for [Modern Records](/wiki/Modern_Records "Modern Records"),[[2]](#cite_note-acerecords1-2) and its follow-up company [Kent Records](/wiki/Kent_Records "Kent Records"), whilst in the 1990s, the company bought the labels including all original master tapes.

## Sublabels

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Ace_Records_(United_Kingdom)&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Sublabels")]

The following labels are owned or licensed by Ace Records.[[3]](#cite_note-3)

* **Ace Records**: initially the only label[[4]](#cite_note-4) (reissues, also including [Ace Records (US)](/wiki/Ace_Records_(US) "Ace Records (US)")): Rock 'n' Roll, Rockabilly, Rhythm & Blues, Cajun, 1950s and 1960s Pop, etc. It is well known for several series of releases: "The Golden Age of American Rock 'N' Roll"; "Early Girls"; "Teen Beat" (showcasing instrumentals); and "Radio Gold".
* Beat Goes Public: Rare and Classic Funk
* [Big Beat](/wiki/Big_Beat_Records_(Ace_subsidiary) "Big Beat Records (Ace subsidiary)"): 1960s Rock 'n' Roll and Pop
* Globe Style: World Music
* [Kent Records](/wiki/Kent_Records "Kent Records"): Motown, Northern Soul, Deep Soul
* [Kicking Mule](/wiki/Kicking_Mule "Kicking Mule"): Guitar Music (Stefan Grossman, Bob Brozman, Bert Jansch)
* Southbound Records: 1970s Soul and Funk ([Millie Jackson](/wiki/Millie_Jackson "Millie Jackson"), Fatback, Sylvester, Joe Simon)
* [Westbound Records](/wiki/Westbound_Records "Westbound Records"): Funk, Soul (Funkadelic, Ohio Players, Detroit Emeralds)
